commands.helpInvocation: --help

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:
  init            Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings
  check           Validate config and check if assets exist
  dev             Compile TypeScript/SCSS for development (once)
  watch           Compile and watch for changes
  build           Build frontend bundles (production)
  help            Show this help message
  version         Show version information

Run 'dotnet frontend help' for detailed options.
Unknown command: development:

commands.command: Development: help Development: version Production: version Production: help
commands.helpInvocation: help Development: help Development: version Production: version Production: help

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:

    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

  dotnet frontend dev

  dotnet frontend check --all

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --verbose     Show detailed build output

    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

commands.payload:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

  dotnet frontend dev

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

commands.payload:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

commands.payload:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

commands.payload:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run
