result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

commands.payload:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

commands.payload:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

commands.payload:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

commands.payload:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.

    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets

    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

    --verbose     Show detailed build output

    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run

result.stdout:
MvcFrontendKit CLI

Usage: dotnet frontend <command> [options]

Commands:

  init [--force]
    Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
    --force       Overwrite existing config file

  check [options]
    Validate configuration and check asset paths.
    --verbose     Show detailed output
    --view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
    --all         Check all discoverable views
    --skip-imports  Skip import path validation

  dev [options]
    Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
    Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  watch [options]
    Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
    Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
    --verbose     Show detailed compilation output

  build [options]
    Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
    Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
    --dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
    --verbose     Show detailed build output

Workflow:
  Development:
    1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
    2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
    Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

  Production:
    Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
    - Compiles TS/SCSS
    - Bundles and minifies all assets
    - Generates fingerprinted filenames
    - Creates frontend.manifest.json

Examples:
  dotnet frontend init
  dotnet frontend dev
  dotnet frontend watch
  dotnet frontend check --all
  dotnet frontend build --dry-run
